items.title: 'Everything's on hold': Patients caught in surgery backlog

children.content: Patients are waiting years for elective surgery as delays and cancellations mount in Tasmania — can the health system handle the $700 million of cuts still to come?

gallery.altText: A person in surgical scrubs, mask and gloves hands a pair of gloves to another person in scrubs without gloves on.

thumbnail.altText: A person in surgical scrubs, mask and gloves hands a pair of gloves to another person in scrubs without gloves on.

items.title: Crossbench MPs demand answers from Tasmanian premier, issue ultimatum

children.content: Seven crossbench MPs have written to Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff to demand answers about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's resignation from the ministry, giving him until Friday morning to provide "direct and exact answers".

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff and Madeleine Ogilvie in parliament.

items.title: Tasmania's population boom has gone bust and it spells trouble

children.content: From being a hot destination 10 years ago, Tasmania's population growth has ground to a halt. Big changes will be needed to reverse the trend, experts say.

image.altText: Ellie Warmbrunn smiles at the camera.

items.title: New mission for volunteers who helped find Celine and Peter

children.content: A group of volunteers helped find the remains of two long-term missing people in Tasmania this year. Now, they are planning to look for Michael Vrankovic, who has not been seen since January.

image.altText: A group of people wearing high visibility clothing search in a line, walking through dry eucalypt forest in the mist.

items.title: Cyclist injured after collision with police vehicle on Tasmania's east coast

children.content: Police say a woman riding a motorised bicycle sustained serious injuries in the crash at St Helens.

image.altText: Tasmania Police vehicles.

items.title: PM pays tribute to 'personal friend' and former neighbour Richard Scolyer

children.content: Loved ones of former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer have paid tribute to his legacy, with his family saying he "wanted cancer patients to know they weren't alone".

articles.title: What is glioblastoma and why is it so hard to treat?

children.content: Glioblastoma claims the lives of 200,000 people each year globally. Despite decades of research and funding, survival rates remain low.

image.altText: An x-ray type image showing a tumour inside a human brain.

articles.title: Richard Scolyer, who challenged the world to 'be brave, be bold', dies aged 59

children.content: The 2024 Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer has died. In his final message he urged all Australians to "pursue their dreams and passion with humility, love and compassion".

image.altText: Tight close-up poortrait of a man with light hair and eyebrows, smiling, wearing a blue-collared shirt. Greenery blurred in back

items.title: Richard Scolyer remembered by peers for contributions to cancer treatment

children.content: Before being diagnosed with cancer, the 2024 Australian of the Year was a leading melanoma researcher, developing better therapies for those with the disease.

articles.title: 'Never be forgotten': Tributes pour in for Richard Scolyer

children.content: The family of former Australian of the Year Richard Scolyer have paid tribute to his legacy, saying he "wanted cancer patients to know they weren't alone".

image.altText: Professor Richard Scolyer during the 2024 Australian of the Year Awards

articles.title: What is glioblastoma and why is it so hard to treat?

children.content: Glioblastoma claims the lives of 200,000 people each year globally. Despite decades of research and funding, survival rates remain low.

image.altText: An x-ray type image showing a tumour inside a human brain.

items.title: Xi and Kim pledge deeper cooperation between China and North Korea

children.content: China and North Korea should strengthen exchanges "in diplomacy, law enforcement, and the military"
Chinese leader Xi Jinping has suggested during a rare two-day visit to Pyongyang.

image.altText: Two men shake hands standing in front of several Chinese and North Korean flags.

items.title: Young couple told by agent that landlords would not rent to 'people like you'

children.content: Hopeful renters say negative stereotypes about young people are making it almost impossible to secure a rental on the Sunshine Coast.

image.altText: A young couple stand outside a house. The man wears tradie yellows. The woman wear's colour nurse's scrubs.

items.title: Four-time veterans to late bolters, here's your Socceroos pocket guide

children.content: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

image.altText: Three soccer players embracae in green and gold.

items.title: Melbourne's data centre boom sparks fire fears

children.content: Serious concerns about an influx of large-scale data centres in Melbourne's west are being raised by Fire Rescue Victoria insiders.

image.altText: Large sign reading Next DC outside a large industrial building with a construction crane in the background

items.title: The most contentious parts of the NDIS bill being scrutinised today

children.content: Politicians are presiding over three days of public hearings into a bill paving the way for the biggest ever cuts to the NDIS.

gallery.altText: A graphic illustration of a person in a wheelchair, an NDIS logo and a pair of scissors

thumbnail.altText: A graphic illustration of a person in a wheelchair, an NDIS logo and a pair of scissors

items.title: As One Nation nips at his heels the PM has no choice but to play the long game

children.content: The government is playing a long game in responding to the surge of support for One Nation. It has little other choice.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese speaks to the media

items.title: At least 35 dead after magnitude-7.8 earthquake hits Philippines

children.content: At least 35 people have been killed in a magnitude-7.8 earthquake that shook the Mindanao region in the southern Philippines, with a tsunami possible on some regional coasts.

image.altText: a collapsed building after a magnitude 7.8 quake in General Santos

items.title: Heated Rivalry meets Yellowface in Josh Silver's debut novel, Fruit Fly

children.content: Described as one of the books of the year, Fruit Fly asks who has the right to tell which stories in an age of cancellation for cultural appropriation.

gallery.altText: A young man with tattoos and shaved, bleach blond hair, wearing a white t-shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A young man with tattoos and shaved, bleach blond hair, wearing a white t-shirt.

items.title: A 'ring of fire' could soon burn around one of Australia's major cities

children.content: Australian states are pushing ahead with waste-to-energy incineration despite significant opposition. It means one capital city will soon be surrounded by a "ring of fire".

image.altText: Protesters holding up signs.

items.title: Your rights and options if you find mould in your rental

children.content: Who is responsible for a mould issue depends on a few factors, but experts say it is worth acting early on.

image.altText: A woman with long black hair staring up at mould on a ceiling

items.title: Undersea volcano inundates island with 'desert' of stone

children.content: Residents are concerned about access to food and fresh water as pumice stone makes travel difficult for Papua New Guinea's seafaring communities.

gallery.altText: Two people with sticks walk along a coastline with stone floating on the ocean behind them
Brown rocks obscure the ocean which laps at a shoreline lined with green trees
A cloud of white smoke steams up from under a deep blue ocean

items.title: Kings and Queens quiz: Where is Prince Harry in the royal line of succession?

children.content: The latest ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to rank some of the most famous (and infamous) royals in history, and sort some of the current crop.

image.altText: Prince Charles grinning while standing between his sons William and Harry

items.title: Mihocek experienced 'lack of feeling' before hospital visit says coach

children.content: Melbourne claims an eight-point win over Collingwood at the MCG as Brody Mihocek is taken to hospital after a serious injury.

image.altText: Billy Frampton calls for a stretcher for Brody Mihocek

items.title: Blues centre Stephen Crichton ruled out of State of Origin II

children.content: New South Wales have been dealt an injury blow ahead of State of Origin with Stephen Crichton ruled out of the squad due to a shoulder injury.

image.altText: A man looks on during a rugby league match

items.title: Script reveals methods of Cambodian centre scamming Australians

children.content: Australian and Thai police release a script discovered in a Cambodian scam compound, revealing the high-pressure methods used to coerce Australian victims.

image.altText: A soldier stands next to barbed wire in the street outside tall multi-storey buildings.

items.title: Sentencing of officer Benedict Bryant a rare moment of accountability

children.content: Inside a quiet district courtroom in the heart of Sydney’s bustling CBD, a historic moment has unfolded involving a NSW Police sergeant.

image.altText: Lachlan is standing beside some stairs holding a photo of Jai in a brown photoframe. He is wearing a white t-shirt

items.title: Australian dollar dives to two-month low as interest rate fears grip markets

children.content: The Australian dollar falls to its lowest level in over two months, just over 70 cents to the US dollar, amid a renewed surge in the greenback as global interest rates, including US interest rates, rise.

image.altText: An Australian dollar coin placed on a US bank note.

items.title: Armenia votes for pro-West party despite pressure from Russia

children.content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's party wins 49.8 per cent of votes, enough to secure a parliamentary majority under Armenia's electoral system.

gallery.altText: A man in a blue suit and blue tie with no hair casts a vote in a ballot box

thumbnail.altText: A man in a blue suit and blue tie with no hair casts a vote in a ballot box

items.title: Rider killed in Finke Desert Race crash identified as Matthew Bielefeld

children.content: A motorcyclist who died while competing in this weekend's Finke Desert Race outside Alice Springs has been identified as Matthew Bielefeld.

image.altText: Two photos of a brunette man smiling, he is alongside a motorbike in one image and standing with a blonde woman in the other.

items.title: Indigenous families want 'crisis' investigated after 15 deaths in six months

children.content: Remote Indigenous communities across Australia are painfully aware that their life expectancy rates are worsening — and in one Northern Territory community, residents are calling for an investigation into a local death rate they feel is "not normal".

image.altText: Aboriginal senior woman and younger Aboriginal man sitting together, serious expression, in front of green leaves.

items.title: Celebrities slip and slide in first Big Freeze event since Neale Daniher's death

children.content: The MCG has hosted the first Big Freeze MND fundraiser since the death of AFL legend and motor neurone disease campaigner, Neale Daniher.

gallery.altText: Weather presenter Sam Mac participates in the Big Freeze event dressed as children's character Bluey

thumbnail.altText: Weather presenter Sam Mac participates in the Big Freeze event dressed as children's character Bluey

items.title: Trump storms out of interview after grilling on 'crooked' election claims

children.content: US President Donald Trump angrily storms out of an interview with American broadcaster NBC after he was questioned about his unfounded claims of election fraud.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and red tie, sitting for an interview

items.title: Calls for kindness as community mourns death of shark attack victim

children.content: The Albany community is still coming to terms with the loss of resident Daniel Turpin after he did not survive a shark attack at the weekend.

image.altText: Compositie image of a man on left and marina on right.

items.title: The 20-year-old Australian named one of the hottest players ahead of the FIFA World Cup

children.content: Nestory Irankunda is set for a breakout moment at the FIFA World Cup as the face of a changing Socceroos team featuring six players of African descent.

gallery.altText: Nestory Irankunda takes a selfie with fans after a Socceroos game.

thumbnail.altText: Nestory Irankunda takes a selfie with fans after a Socceroos game.
